untranslatable_reason: Broadening Bottom pattern requires trendline detection, diverging boundary analysis, and alternating touch counting. These are visual/geometric pattern elements not expressible through the available indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, Volume, Position, Bar Properties). Trendline slope calculation, touch point identification, and divergence measurement require candlestick-level pattern recognition beyond the scope of indicator-based conditions.
translation_notes: Signal describes a chart pattern (Broadening Bottom) that requires visual trendline analysis and geometric pattern recognition. The entry condition 'approaches top of formation heading toward breakout' and the concept of 'alternating touches to diverging trendlines' cannot be reliably operationalized using only the allowed indicator set. A practical approximation using volatility (ATR) and price position (SMA) has been provided, but this does not capture the true pattern mechanics described by Bulkowski.
